nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Global Health Devel & Diversit
nodes.description: An anthropological exploration of connections between global health, economic development, and cultural diversity in contemporary times. Considers ways in which informed global citizens can make a positive difference in human health, taking socioeconomic and cultural diversity into account. Cross-listed with: HSOC 1700.

nodes.title: Human Osteology
nodes.description: An exploration of the human skeleton as a means of reconstructing past lives both at the level of individuals (forensics) and populations (archaeology and bioarchaeology). Prerequisites: ANTH 1800, ANTH 1400, one 2000-level Anthropology course in archaeology or biological anthropology, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Psychological Anthropology
nodes.description: Explores the contributions of anthropology to understanding the experience of psychological phenomena and mind across cultures. It will be a holistic introduction to the vibrant field of psychological anthropology, and it is designed to challenge preconceived notions about how culture affects people's behavior. Prerequisites: ANTH 1100 or ANTH 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: FYS: Gend Sex Wmn's Stdy
nodes.description: Intensive first-year seminar introducing the field of Gender, Sexuality, and Women's Studies. Topics include key theoretical approaches to conceptualizing gender, sexuality, and power; how gender and sexuality are policed; and the relationship between gender, sexuality, and other social categories. Emphasis on developing critical reading and writing skills, substantive revision, information literacy, and analytical thinking.

nodes.title: Landscape Archaeology
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. Prerequisites: ANTH 1100, one 2000-level course.

nodes.title: Linguistic Anthropology
nodes.description: Introduction to linguistic anthropology, focusing on language and communication as they pertain to human culture and human social interaction.

nodes.title: Archaeological Theory
nodes.description: Development of archaeology from the nineteenth century to the present including concepts of form, space and time, intellectual attitudes, current systems theory, and research strategies. Prerequisites: ANTH 1800 and one 2000-level Anthropology course; or HP 5201; or Historic Preservation Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Historic Preservation Graduate student

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Advanced Proseminar in Anthro
nodes.description: Designed to be taken in conjunction with any 3000-level class, this capstone pro-seminar in Anthropology will provide a forum for majors to build and package anthropological skill sets and to identify, explore, and plan for future educational and career opportunities. Prerequisites: Anthropology major; minimum Junior standing. Pre/co-requisite: Any three-credit 3000-level Anthropology course.
nodes.other_requirements: Anthropology major; minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: Culture, Health and Healing
nodes.description: Introduction to medical anthropology. Social and cultural perspectives on health and illness experiences, doctor-patient interactions, healing practices, and access to health and health care. Prerequisites: ANTH 1100, ANTH 1190, or three hours of Sociology.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Minimum Junior standing; Instructor permission
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; Instructor permission

nodes.title: Cultural Anthropology
nodes.description: Introduction to cultural anthropology, using fieldwork-based concepts and methods to study diverse cultural views and practices, varied forms of social organization, and contemporary global issues.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Primates and Anthropology
nodes.description: A survey of behavior and anatomy of nonhuman primates (monkeys, apes and prosimians) from an anthropological perspective. Prerequisite: ANTH 1100 or ANTH 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Anthropology of Food and Labor
nodes.description: Unpacks the political, cultural, and economic dimensions of both local and global food systems through an investigation of the ways in which people work through, around, and with food in the public sphere. Prerequisites: ANTH 1100; one course at the 2000-level in cultural anthropology.
nodes.other_requirements: one course at the 2000-level in cultural anthropology

nodes.title: Archaeology Headlines
nodes.description: Explores intermediate level topics in archaeology. May be repeated for credit with different content. Sample topics include: Archaeology of Disaster, Ruins, Archaeology in the Media. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: ANTH 1800.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Black Feminist Anthropology
nodes.description: Explores intermediate level topics in cultural anthropology. May be repeated for credit with different content. Sample topics include: Cultural Anthropology in the Media, Indigenous Cultures and Social Change, Economic Anthropology. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: ANTH 1100.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Digital Anthropology
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. Special Topics courses cannot carry CC designations

nodes.title: Archaeological Anthropology
nodes.description: Examination of the origins and development of culture from the earliest human fossils through the appearance of civilization; the nature of archaeological data and interpretations.

nodes.title: Introduction to the Major
nodes.description: Seminar-style introduction to the Anthropology major focusing on skill-building, course selection, internships, service learning, research or teaching assistantships, study abroad, fieldwork, senior projects/theses, and grant opportunities. Prerequisites: Anthropology major and one of the following: ANTH 1100, ANTH 1800, ANTH 1400, or ANTH 1600.
nodes.other_requirements: Anthropology major and First year or sophomore ANTH majors only

nodes.title: Ethnographic Field Methods
nodes.description: Examination of theoretical and ethical premises of field work methodology with practical experience in research design, proposal writing, participant observation, interviewing, and qualitative data analysis. Prerequisite: ANTH 1100, one course at the 2000-level in cultural anthropology.
nodes.other_requirements: one course at the 2000-level in cultural anthropology.

nodes.title: Fndns of Global Health
nodes.description: Explores global health and global health challenges affecting people primarily in developing or resource-constrained countries. Prerequisite: Minimum Sophomore standing. Cross-listed with: ANTH 2191.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing. HSCI, PHS, and MRS majors only; Degree students only;

nodes.title: Intro to GSWS
nodes.description: Introduction to the field of gender, sexuality, and women's studies. Topics include key theoretical approaches to conceptualizing gender, sexuality, and power; how gender and sexuality are policed; and the relationship between gender, sexuality, and other social categories. Credit not awarded for both GSWS 1500 and GSWS 1010.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Foundations of Global Health
nodes.description: Explores global health and global health challenges affecting people primarily in developing or resource-constrained countries. Prerequisite: Minimum Sophomore standing. Cross-listed with: HSCI 2100.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing. ANTH and HSOC majors only during the week of registration, then ANTH and HSOC minors may also enroll; Degree students only; Cross listed listed with HSCI 2100A; Total combined enrollment: 60

nodes.title: Food and Culture
nodes.description: Examination of the cultivation, preparation, and consumption of food as rich symbolic processes through which humans interact with our natural and social environments.

nodes.title: Gender Sex Race & the Body
nodes.description: Cross-cultural study of gender, sex, sexuality, and race including exploring the cultural construction of categories and cultural practices related to the body and gender, sex, sexuality, and race. Prerequisite: ANTH 1100, GSWS 1010, or GSWS 1500. Cross-listed with: GSWS 2715.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Political Anthropology
nodes.description: Exploration of the cultural aspects of political institutions, structures, and processes in societies from around the world. Prerequisite: ANTH 1100.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Ruins
nodes.description: Examination of ruins--abandoned places and things--through an anthropological lens. The survey ranges broadly from ancient sites to contemporary war zones, abandoned malls, and other familiar landscapes, with critical attention to the portrayal of ruins in film, photography, and other media.
